member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.CryptoHelper
member.summary:
Provides AES-256 encryption and decryption with PBKDF2 key derivation. Shared by Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem and Expand-PSDataRepositoryItem cmdlets.
V4 format (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA512, 210k iterations, default): [PSDR-ENC-V4 11B][Salt 32B][Nonce 12B][Tag 16B][Ciphertext]
V3 format (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA256, 600k iterations, legacy read): [PSDR-ENC-V3 11B][Salt 32B][Nonce 12B][Tag 16B][Ciphertext]
V2 format (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA256, 100k iterations, legacy read): [PSDR-ENC-V2 11B][Salt 32B][Nonce 12B][Tag 16B][Ciphertext]
V1 format (AES-CBC, legacy read-only): [PSDR-ENC-V1 11B][Salt 32B][IV 16B][Ciphertext]

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.CryptoHelper.HasEncryptionHeader(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Checks whether the data starts with any known encryption header (V1–V4).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.CryptoHelper.Encrypt(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Encrypts data using AES-256-GCM (V4) with a PBKDF2-SHA512-derived key (210k iterations). Provides authenticated encryption — ciphertext integrity is verified on decryption.
member.param: Plaintext bytes to encrypt. The caller is responsible for clearing this buffer after the call (e.g. via ); because byte[] is passed by reference, this method does not own the buffer and cannot decide when it is safe to wipe.
member.returns: Encrypted payload including V4 header, salt, nonce, tag, and ciphertext.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.CryptoHelper.Decrypt(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Decrypts data encrypted by . Supports V4 (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA512 210k), V3 (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA256 600k), V2 (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA256 100k) and V1 (AES-CBC, legacy) formats.
member.param: Encrypted payload including header, salt, and ciphertext.
Password used for key derivation (must match encryption).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.CryptoHelper.DecryptGcm(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte},System.Int32,System.Security.Cryptography.HashAlgorithmName)
member.summary: Decrypts AES-GCM payload (V2, V3 or V4). Format: [Header 11B][Salt 32B][Nonce 12B][Tag 16B][Ciphertext]

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.CryptoHelper.DecryptV1(System.Byte[],System.Security.SecureString)
member.summary: Decrypts V1 (AES-CBC) payload. Retained for backward compatibility with existing encrypted data. Format: [PSDR-ENC-V1 11B][Salt 32B][IV 16B][Ciphertext]

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants
member.summary: Shared encryption constants used by Compress and Expand cmdlets. Centralizing these ensures Compress and Expand always use matching parameters, preventing data loss from accidental desynchronization.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.AesKeySize
member.summary: AES key size in bits (256-bit / 32 bytes).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.AesBlockSize
member.summary: AES block size in bits (128-bit / 16 bytes).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.SaltSize
member.summary: Salt size in bytes for PBKDF2 key derivation (256 bits).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.IVSize
member.summary: IV size in bytes for AES-CBC mode (V1 only).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.Pbkdf2IterationsLegacy
member.summary: Number of PBKDF2 iterations for key derivation (V1 and V2 data). Retained for backward-compatible decryption of existing encrypted data.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.Pbkdf2Iterations
member.summary: Number of PBKDF2 iterations for key derivation (V3+). OWASP 2023 recommends 600,000 for SHA-256.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.Pbkdf2IterationsV4
member.summary: Number of PBKDF2 iterations for key derivation with SHA-512 (V4). OWASP 2023 recommends 210,000 for SHA-512 (lower than SHA-256 because each SHA-512 iteration is more expensive on a defender CPU but proportionally faster on a 64-bit attacker GPU/ASIC, so iteration counts are not directly comparable).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.EncryptionMagicHeader
member.summary: Magic header bytes for V1 (AES-CBC) encrypted files. Immutable via ReadOnlySpan.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.EncryptionMagicHeaderV2
member.summary: Magic header bytes for V2 (AES-GCM, 100k iterations) encrypted files. Immutable via ReadOnlySpan.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.EncryptionMagicHeaderV3
member.summary: Magic header bytes for V3 (AES-GCM, 600k iterations) encrypted files. Immutable via ReadOnlySpan.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.EncryptionMagicHeaderV4
member.summary: Magic header bytes for V4 (AES-GCM, PBKDF2-SHA512, 210k iterations) encrypted files. Immutable via ReadOnlySpan.

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.GcmNonceSize
member.summary: AES-GCM nonce size in bytes (96 bits, NIST recommendation).

member.name: F:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.EncryptionConstants.GcmTagSize
member.summary: AES-GCM authentication tag size in bytes (128 bits).

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.ErrorIds
member.summary:
Centralized error ID constants used by all PSDataRepository cmdlets.
Error IDs are part of the public contract surfaced via PowerShell's $Error[0].FullyQualifiedErrorId, so callers (CI scripts, monitoring, other modules) can switch on them. Centralising the strings here prevents typos and ensures the same logical condition raises the same ID across cmdlets.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.FormatterNameCompleter
member.summary: Provides tab-completion for formatter names (Json, Xml, Csv, Yml, etc.) by querying the at runtime.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.ProviderNameCompleter
member.summary: Provides tab-completion for provider names (Disk, AzureBlob, AzureKeyVault, ...) by querying the at runtime.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.PSDataRepositoryCmdletBase
member.summary: Base class for PSDataRepository cmdlets that want cooperative cancellation via Ctrl-C / PowerShell pipeline stop. Derived cmdlets should pass to async operations (typically through AsyncHelper.RunSync(..., CancellationToken)).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.PSDataRepositoryCmdletBase.CancellationToken
member.summary: Cancellation token that is signaled when the pipeline is stopped (Ctrl-C).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.Common.PSDataRepositoryCmdletBase.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Releases managed resources (the cancellation source).

summary.para: Compresses and optionally encrypts items in persistent storage.

Compresses stored items using GZip compression to reduce storage size. Optionally encrypts compressed data using AES-256 encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation. The compressed item is saved with .gz extension (or .gz.enc if encrypted). Supports in-place compression or creating a new compressed copy. Ideal for archiving large datasets or reducing storage costs with security.

example.para: Compresses largefile.json to largefile.json.gz and removes original.
Compresses and encrypts sensitive.json with password protection.
Compresses all log files, keeping originals.
Compresses data.json to specific destination.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the item to compress.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DestinationName
member.summary: Destination name for compressed item. If not specified, adds .gz extension to original name. If password is used, adds .gz.enc extension.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Password
member.summary: Password for AES-256 encryption. If specified, compressed data will be encrypted. Use Read-Host -AsSecureString to securely prompt for password.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CompressionLevel
member.summary: Compression level: Optimal (default), Fastest, or NoCompression.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.KeepOriginal
member.summary: If specified, keeps the original item after compression.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, overwrites existing compressed item without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.PassThru
member.summary: If specified, returns information about the compressed item.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CompressPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DetermineDestinationName
member.summary: Determines the destination name based on parameters and encryption status.

summary.para: Connects to a data repository provider.

Establishes a session to a data repository provider. The Provider parameter selects which provider to use. Available providers and their parameters are discovered dynamically from registered plugins. Authentication parameters are added automatically based on the provider's supported auth modes and the loaded authentication plugins.

example.para: Connects to a local disk-based repository.
Connects to Azure Blob Storage using a connection string.

summary.para: The data repository provider type to connect to. Available providers are discovered from loaded plugins.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConnectPSDataRepositoryCommand.GetDynamicParameters
member.summary: Returns dynamic parameters based on the selected Provider. Parameters come from the provider definition and authentication plugins.

summary.para: Deserializes a raw message string into a PowerShell object.

Converts a raw message string (e.g. received from an Azure Function Queue trigger) into a deserialized object using the module's Formatter. Does not require an active session or queue connection. Automatically detects the serialization format (JSON, XML, CSV) unless -Format is specified. If the message was sent with -IncludeMetadata, the metadata envelope is automatically unwrapped and the inner content is deserialized. Use -IncludeMetadata to attach the envelope metadata as note properties on the output object.

example.para: Deserializes a message received from an Azure Function Queue trigger.
Explicitly deserializes a JSON message from pipeline input.
Deserializes a message and includes envelope metadata (TypeName, Timestamp, etc.) as note properties.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.InputObject
member.summary: The raw message string to deserialize. Accepts pipeline input.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Format
member.summary: Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMetadata
member.summary: If specified, includes metadata properties from the envelope (TypeName, Timestamp, etc.) as note properties on the deserialized object. Only applies when the message contains a metadata envelope (sent with -IncludeMetadata).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ConvertFromPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.TryUnwrapEnvelope(System.String,System.String@,System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement}@,System.String@)
member.summary: Attempts to detect and unwrap a metadata envelope created by Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage -IncludeMetadata.

summary.para: Copies local files or repository items to the connected repository.

Uploads local files (from disk) to the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports pipeline input from Get-ChildItem (binds via FullName property) and from Get-PSDataRepositoryChildItem (intra-repository copy). When copying from the local filesystem, the cmdlet reads each file as binary and stores it under the original file name (or a custom -Destination prefix). Supports -Recurse for directory traversal, -Filter for wildcard matching, and -Flatten to discard folder structure.

example.para: Copies a single file to the repository root.
Recursively copies all files from the directory to the repository.
Pipes local files into the repository (binds FullName automatically).
Copies filtered files to a subfolder in the repository.
Copies items within the repository from one prefix to another.
Copies all files without preserving directory structure.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Path
member.summary: Local file or directory path(s) to copy to the repository. Accepts pipeline input from Get-ChildItem via the FullName property.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.LiteralPath
member.summary: Literal local path(s) without wildcard expansion.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.InputObject
member.summary: Repository item received from Get-PSDataRepositoryChildItem. Performs intra-repository copy to the specified -Destination.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Destination
member.summary: Destination prefix or folder in the repository. If omitted, items are copied to the repository root preserving their relative names.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Recurse
member.summary: Recursively copy files from directories.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Filter
member.summary: Wildcard filter pattern applied to file names (e.g. "*.json").

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: Overwrite existing items in the repository without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.PassThru
member.summary: Return information about each copied item.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Flatten
member.summary: Discard directory structure and use only leaf file names as destination names.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: Continue processing when individual copy operations fail.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CopyLocalPath(System.String)
member.summary: Resolves a local path and copies the file(s) to the repository. Handles single files, directories, and wildcard patterns.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CopyDirectory(System.String,System.String,System.IO.SearchOption)
member.summary: Enumerates files in a directory and copies each to the repository.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CopyLocalFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Copies a single local file to the repository. Uses streaming via when supported to avoid loading large files entirely into memory.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CopyRepositoryItem(PSDataRepository.Abstractions.RepositoryItemInfo)
member.summary: Copies a repository item to a new destination within the same repository.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DetermineDestinationName(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Determines the destination name in the repository for a local file.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DetermineRepositoryDestinationName(PSDataRepository.Abstractions.RepositoryItemInfo)
member.summary: Determines the destination name for an intra-repository copy.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.GetLeafName(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the leaf (file) name from a path. For "data/config.json" returns "config.json".

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.CopyPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ApplyFilter(System.String)
member.summary: Applies the -Filter wildcard pattern to a file path. Returns true when the file should be included.

summary.para: Disconnects from the current data repository session.

Closes the active repository connection established by Connect-PSDataRepository. Disposes all underlying resources (storage clients, queue clients, cached connections) and clears the session state. Returns $true on success, $false if an error occurred during cleanup. Safe to call even when no session is active.

example.para: Disconnects from the current repository and releases all resources.
Typical connect-work-disconnect workflow.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.DisconnectPSDataRepositoryCommand.ProcessRecord
member.summary: Disconnects from the currently active repository and clears the session.

summary.para: Decompresses and optionally decrypts items in persistent storage.

Decompresses GZip-compressed items stored in the repository. Automatically decrypts items encrypted with Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem if password is provided. Automatically detects .gz extension and removes it from decompressed item name. Supports in-place decompression or creating a new decompressed copy. Ideal for restoring archived datasets or processing compressed/encrypted data.

example.para: Decompresses largefile.json.gz to largefile.json and removes compressed file.
Decrypts and decompresses encrypted file with password.
Decompresses all .gz files, keeping compressed originals.
Decompresses to specific destination.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the compressed item to expand.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DestinationName
member.summary: Destination name for decompressed item. If not specified, removes .gz extension from original name.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Password
member.summary: Password for AES-256 decryption. Required if item was encrypted with Compress-PSDataRepositoryItem. Use Read-Host -AsSecureString to securely prompt for password.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.KeepOriginal
member.summary: If specified, keeps the compressed item after decompression.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, overwrites existing decompressed item without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ExpandPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.PassThru
member.summary: If specified, returns information about the decompressed item.

summary.para: Lists child items (metadata only) from the connected repository.

Returns strongly-typed objects describing files, blobs, or virtual directories in the connected repository. Unlike Get-PSDataRepositoryItem, this cmdlet does NOT download or deserialize content — it returns metadata such as Name, Size, LastModified, ContentType, and Extension. Supports -Recurse for recursive listing and -Filter for wildcard matching.

example.para: Lists all items at the root of the connected repository.
Lists items in the 'data' subfolder.
Lists all items recursively from the root.
Lists only items matching the wildcard pattern.
Lists only directories/virtual folders.
Lists only files recursively.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.Path
member.summary: Subfolder or prefix to scope the listing. Defaults to the repository root.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.Filter
member.summary: Wildcard filter applied to item names (e.g. "*.json").

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.Recurse
member.summary: When specified, includes items from all nested subdirectories.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.Directory
member.summary: When specified, returns only directories (virtual folders).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.File
member.summary: When specified, returns only files (non-directory items).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.Depth
member.summary: Maximum directory depth for recursive listing (0 = only direct children).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.GetLeafName(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the leaf (file/folder) name from a relative path. For "data/config.json" returns "config.json". For "data/" returns "data".

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryChildItemCommand.CountDepth(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Counts the directory depth of relative to .

summary.para: Reads the strong-name public key token from a .NET assembly in the format used by extensions.trust.json.

Helper for administrators who need to add a 3rd-party PSDataRepository extension to the trust list. Reads the assembly metadata without loading it for execution and returns the lowercase 16-character hex public key token, suitable for pasting into extensions.trust.json under trustedPublicKeyTokens.

example.para: Prints the assembly's public key token (e.g. a1b2c3d4e5f60708).
Pipeline-friendly: emits one record per assembly with Path, Name and Token.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryExtensionTokenCommand.Path
member.summary: Path to the assembly file to inspect. Accepts pipeline input by value or by property name (e.g. from Get-ChildItem).

summary.para: Retrieves items from persistent storage (Blob, Disk).

Loads and deserializes items from the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports retrieving single items, multiple items by pattern, or listing all items. Items are automatically deserialized based on format (JSON, XML, CSV) or content detection. Ideal for loading configuration, datasets, or processing results.

example.para: Retrieves and deserializes config.json.
Retrieves all JSON files from the data folder.
Explicitly deserializes as XML.
Returns raw content without deserialization.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key or pattern of the object(s) to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) depending on provider.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ListAll
member.summary: If specified, lists all available objects in the repository.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Format
member.summary: Expected deserialization format. If not specified, auto-detects from content or file extension.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Encoding
member.summary: Text encoding for reading. Default: UTF-8. Accepts encoding names (UTF8, ASCII, Unicode, etc.) with tab-completion.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Raw
member.summary: If specified, returns raw content as string without deserialization.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.AsByteArray
member.summary: If specified, returns content as byte array (binary mode).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.IncludeMetadata
member.summary: If specified, includes metadata (Name, Path, Size) as properties on output objects.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual object retrieval fails.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ListAllObjects
member.summary: Lists all objects in the repository.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.RetrieveObject(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves a single object by name.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ApplyDefaultDisplayPropertySet(System.Management.Automation.PSObject,System.String)
member.summary: Hides the synthetic property from default Format-* output by attaching a PSStandardMembers.DefaultDisplayPropertySet containing only the object's own (non-excluded, non-PS*) properties. The excluded property remains accessible by name (so pipeline binding by property name still works).

summary.para: Lists all supported PSDataRepository providers and their capabilities.

Displays information about each data repository provider the module can work with, including provider type, supported capabilities (Storage, Queue, Secrets), authentication methods, and connection examples. Does not require an active session.

example.para: Lists all available providers with their capabilities.
Shows details for a specific provider.
Lists only providers that support queue messaging.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryProviderCommand.Name
member.summary: Filter by provider name. If not specified, all providers are listed.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositoryProviderCommand.Capability
member.summary: Filter by capability (Storage, Queue, Secrets).

summary.para: Retrieves a secret value from the connected repository.
Gets the value of a secret from the connected repository (typically Azure Key Vault or FileSystem). Fails if the repository type does not support secrets or is not connected.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Name
member.summary: The name of the secret to retrieve.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.GetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.ProcessRecord
member.summary: Processes the cmdlet and retrieves the specified secret from the repository.

summary.para: Retrieves information about the current repository session.
Displays details about the active PSDataRepository session including connection status, provider type, capabilities, and repository identity.

example.para: Displays current session information.

member.name: T:PSDataRepository.Commands.ModuleInitializer
member.summary: Module initializer that runs when the module assembly is loaded into PowerShell. Discovers and registers provider, authentication, and formatter plugins.
member.remarks: This class must live in the assembly loaded via Import-Module (Commands.dll), because PowerShell only invokes on the directly imported assembly, not on transitive dependencies.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ModuleInitializer.OnRemove(System.Management.Automation.PSModuleInfo)
member.remarks: Invoked by PowerShell on Remove-Module. Mirrors the cleanup performed by the OnRemove handler in PSDataRepository.psm1 so the module is also tidy when imported directly via the binary path (tests, MCP host).

summary.para: Moves local files into the repository or moves repository items within the repository.
Supports pipeline input from Get-ChildItem for local files and from Get-PSDataRepositoryChildItem for intra-repository moves.
Receives and deserializes messages from the connected queue repository.

Retrieves messages from the active queue repository and deserializes them back to objects. Supports batching for efficient retrieval of large message volumes. Can operate in single-message mode, batch mode, or continuous polling mode. Messages are automatically acknowledged/deleted after successful deserialization by default.

example.para: Receives up to 10 messages and returns them as objects.
Continuously polls for messages, retrieving up to 100 per batch with 5-second delay between polls.
Peeks at messages without removing them from the queue.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MaxMessages
member.summary: Maximum number of messages to receive in one call. Default: 1.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.VisibilityTimeoutSeconds
member.summary: Visibility timeout for received messages (how long they remain invisible to other consumers). Default: 30 seconds.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Format
member.summary: Deserialization format. If not specified, attempts auto-detection based on content.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Peek
member.summary: If specified, peeks messages without removing them from the queue (read-only).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Raw
member.summary: If specified, returns raw message content without deserialization.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.NoAutoDelete
member.summary: If specified, does not automatically delete/acknowledge messages after processing. Useful for manual acknowledgement scenarios or debugging.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Continuous
member.summary: If specified, continues polling continuously until interrupted (Ctrl+C).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.DelaySeconds
member.summary: Delay in seconds between continuous polling attempts. Default: 5 seconds. Only used with -Continuous.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MaxIterations
member.summary: Maximum number of continuous polling iterations. Default: unlimited (until Ctrl+C). Only used with -Continuous.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual message deserialization fails. Failed messages are written as errors but don't stop processing.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MessageId
member.summary: The ID of a specific message to retrieve. When specified, only the message with the matching ID is returned (using peek, without removing from queue).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMetadata
member.summary: If specified, includes message metadata (Id, Receipt, DequeueCount, timestamps) as properties on output objects.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ReceiveById
member.summary: Receives a single message by its ID using peek (non-destructive).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ReceiveContinuous
member.summary: Receives messages in continuous polling mode.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ReceiveBatch
member.summary: Receives a single batch of messages.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ProcessMessage(PSDataRepository.Abstractions.QueueMessage)
member.summary: Processes a single message: deserialize, output, and optionally delete.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.DeserializeMessage(System.String)
member.summary: Deserializes message content to an object.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.ReceivePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.WaitInterruptible(System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: Waits for the specified duration but returns immediately when Ctrl+C triggers .

summary.para: Removes items from persistent storage (Blob, Disk).
Deletes items from the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports removing single or multiple items by name.

example.para: Removes temp.json from storage.
Removes all JSON files in backup folder without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the item(s) to remove.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.InputObject
member.summary: Repository item received from Get-PSDataRepositoryChildItem. Directories are automatically skipped.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual object deletion fails.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryItemCommand.RemoveItem(System.String)
member.summary: Removes a single item by name with existence check, ShouldProcess, and ShouldContinue logic.

summary.para: Removes/deletes messages from the queue.

Deletes messages from the connected queue repository (InMemory, Disk, Azure Queue, Service Bus). Typically used after successful message processing to prevent reprocessing. Messages must have been received first (to obtain receipt handles). For Azure providers, uses receipt handle for deletion. For Disk/InMemory, uses message ID.

example.para: Receives and then deletes a message.
Receives and deletes multiple messages via pipeline.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Message
member.summary: The message object(s) to delete. Typically obtained from Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.InputObject
member.summary: Pipeline input from Receive-PSDataRepositoryMessage (PSObject with __QueueMessage property).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, suppresses confirmation prompts.

summary.para: Deletes a secret from the connected repository.
Removes (deletes) a secret from the connected repository (typically Azure Key Vault or FileSystem). Fails if the repository type does not support secrets or is not connected.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the secret to remove.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.RemovePSDataRepositorySecretCommand.ProcessRecord
member.summary: Removes the specified secret from the connected repository.

summary.para: Saves repository items to the local file system.

Downloads items from the connected repository and writes them to the local file system. Supports pipeline input from Get-PSDataRepositoryChildItem and preserves repository folder structure by default.
Sends objects to the connected queue repository.

Serializes and sends objects to the active queue repository (InMemory, Disk, Azure Queue, Service Bus). Supports pipeline input for processing large volumes of objects efficiently. Objects are serialized to JSON by default, with configurable format (XML, CSV). Uses batching for optimal performance (default batch size: 100 messages).

example.code: $tasks = @([PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 1; Action = "Backup"; Target = "DB01" }; [PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 2; Action = "Restart"; Target = "App02" }) $tasks | Send-PSDataRepositoryMessage
example.para: Sends a collection of objects to the queue.
Sends 100,000 custom objects with batching of 500 messages.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.InputObject
member.summary: The object(s) to send. Accepts pipeline input.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Format
member.summary: Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, or Csv.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.BatchSize
member.summary: Batch size for sending messages. Default: 100. Larger batches improve throughput but use more memory.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.MaxDepth
member.summary: Maximum serialization depth to prevent infinite recursion. Default: 10.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.CsvDelimiter
member.summary: CSV delimiter (only used if Format = Csv). Default: comma (,).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.XmlRootName
member.summary: XML root element name (only used if Format = Xml). If not specified (null), automatically inferred from object type: - Single object: type name (e.g., 'Process') - Collection: type name + 'List' (e.g., 'ProcessList') Default: null (auto-detect).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMetadata
member.summary: If specified, includes metadata envelope with type information and timestamp.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.IncludeMachineInfo
member.summary: If specified, includes machine name and user name in the metadata envelope. Only used with -IncludeMetadata. Disabled by default to prevent information disclosure.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.ContinueOnError
member.summary: If specified, continues processing even if individual message serialization fails. Failed messages are written as errors but don't stop the pipeline.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.SerializeObject(System.Management.Automation.PSObject)
member.summary: Serializes an object using the configured format.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.CreateMetadataEnvelope(System.Management.Automation.PSObject,System.String)
member.summary: Creates a metadata envelope around the serialized content.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SendPSDataRepositoryMessageCommand.FlushBatch
member.summary: Flushes the current batch buffer to the queue repository.

summary.para: Saves items to persistent storage (Blob, Disk).

Serializes and stores items in the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Supports pipeline input for batch processing and multiple serialization formats. Items are stored as individual files/blobs with specified names. Ideal for saving configuration, datasets, or processing results.

example.code: $data = @([PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 1; Name = "Server01"; Status = "Running" }; [PSCustomObject]@{ Id = 2; Name = "Server02"; Status = "Stopped" }) $data | Set-PSDataRepositoryItem -Name "servers.json"
example.para: Saves a collection of objects as JSON to storage.
Saves configuration hashtable as JSON.
Saves each file item with unique name based on FullName property.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.InputObject
member.summary: The object to save. Accepts pipeline input.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key for storing the object. Can include {0} placeholder for pipeline index. Examples: "data.json", "item-{0}.json", "backup/{0}.xml"

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.NameProperty
member.summary: Property name to use for generating unique names from pipeline objects. Example: -NameProperty "Id" uses each object's Id property as the name.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.NamePrefix
member.summary: Prefix to add before generated names (used with -NameProperty).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.NameSuffix
member.summary: Suffix/extension to add after generated names (used with -NameProperty).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Format
member.summary: Serialization format: Json (default), Xml, Csv, or Yml.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Encoding
member.summary: Text encoding for writing. Default: UTF-8. Accepts encoding names (UTF8, ASCII, Unicode, etc.) with tab-completion.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.MaxDepth
member.summary: Maximum serialization depth. Default: 10.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.CsvDelimiter
member.summary: CSV delimiter character. Only used when Format = Csv. Default: comma (,).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.XmlRootName
member.summary: XML root element name. Only used when Format = Xml. If not specified (null), automatically inferred from object type: - Single object: type name (e.g., 'Process') - Collection: type name + 'List' (e.g., 'ProcessList') Default: null (auto-detect).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, overwrites existing objects without confirmation.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.PassThru
member.summary: If specified, returns the storage path/URI after saving.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Accumulate
member.summary: If specified, accumulates all pipeline objects and saves them as a collection (array). Useful for creating single JSON/XML/CSV file from multiple pipeline objects. Without this, each pipeline object is saved separately (potentially overwriting the same file).

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.DetermineStorageName(System.Management.Automation.PSObject)
member.summary: Determines the storage name based on parameter set.

member.name: M:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.SerializeObject(System.Object)
member.summary: Serializes an object using the configured format. Supports both single objects and arrays.

summary.para: Creates or updates a secret in the connected repository (Key Vault, FileSystem).
Sets (creates or updates) a secret in the connected repository. Supports either plain text or secure string as the secret value. Supported repositories: AzureKeyVault, FileSystem.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Name
member.summary: The name of the secret to set.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Value
member.summary: The plain text value to store as the secret (alternative to SecureValue).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.SecureValue
member.summary: The secure string value to store as the secret (alternative to Value).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.SetPSDataRepositorySecretCommand.Force
member.summary: If specified, skips confirmation prompts.

summary.para: Tests the connectivity of the current repository session.
Performs a lightweight connectivity check against the active repository. Returns $true if the connection is healthy, $false otherwise.

example.para: Tests whether the current session is connected and responsive.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.TestPSDataRepositoryConnectionCommand.TimeoutSeconds
member.summary: Timeout in seconds for the connection test.

summary.para: Tests if an object exists in the repository.
Checks whether one or more objects exist in the connected repository (Azure Blob Storage, Disk). Returns $true if exists, $false otherwise.

example.para: Returns $true if config.json exists, $false otherwise.
Conditional execution based on existence.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.TestPSDataRepositoryItemCommand.Name
member.summary: The name/key of the object to test.

summary.para: Rotates the master passphrase of the connected secret repository.
Re-encrypts every secret stored in the connected repository under a fresh master key derived from the supplied . Currently supported only by the Disk provider (file-system secret store).

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.UpdatePSDataRepositoryMasterPassphraseCommand.NewPassphrase
member.summary: The new passphrase used to derive the master key.

member.name: P:PSDataRepository.Commands.UpdatePSDataRepositoryMasterPassphraseCommand.Force
member.summary: Skip confirmation prompts.
